macro_summary: AI infrastructure spending remains the dominant macro theme, confirmed by TSM's 77% earnings gain and ongoing HBM memory shortages expected to persist beyond 2030. A sector-wide pullback on AI capex valuation fears has created re-entry dislocations in semis and mega-cap tech ahead of a dense earnings window (MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30, NVDA August 26). The Fed's hawkish posture remains a macro headwind but recurring-revenue tech models are proving resilient, while fresh analyst initiations and ATH momentum in AAPL suggest institutional conviction is intact.
screening_note: 6 total overnight screening runs analyzed. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 4 qualifying ideas appeared in 6/6 runs, indicating unusually high overnight consensus across independent screens.
key_events: AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings: July 30, 2026 after close (13 days out)
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings: July 29, 2026 after close (12 days out)
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 26, 2026 after close (40 days out)
SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) fresh Nasdaq listing: July 10, 2026; Barclays Overweight initiation July 14
iPhone 18 cycle begins September 2026 (97% Polymarket probability)
South Korea record AI budget announced week of July 10

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes, the strongest consensus signal of the digest. Stock hit a new ATH near $334 on July 16 with earnings confirmed July 30 AMC — 13 days away. A 4-consecutive-quarter EPS beat streak, fresh China AI approval, and Citi's raised $365 target all support momentum, but the stock has outrun the Street's average consensus target (~$314), making a defined-risk bull call spread the preferred expression over outright stock.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; iPhone 18 cycle launch September 2026 (97% Polymarket probability)
ideas.edge: 4 consecutive EPS beats; consensus EPS $1.88–$1.89 (+19.8–20% YoY); Citi raised target to $365 from $315; stock at ~$327–334 vs. avg Street target ~$314 — stock has outrun consensus, creating IV expansion opportunity pre-earnings with defined-risk structure
ideas.bull_case: Beat streak continues, China AI approval adds an undiscounted revenue layer, and iPhone 18 pre-cycle excitement drives multiple expansion toward Citi's $365 target into and through the print.
ideas.bear_case: Stock trading above average analyst consensus target at elevated multiples; any guidance miss or softer China commentary could trigger a sharp post-ATH reversal, making outright long stock risky.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 4 high-conviction votes, and presents a rare valuation dislocation: trading at 21–22x NTM earnings — the cheapest forward multiple since 2023 — against a 97-analyst average price target of ~$590 vs. a current price near $400, implying ~47% upside. Post-Q4 print (reported July 13) showed 18% revenue growth and Azure + Copilot monetization confirmation; next earnings July 29 provides a near-term binary with AI ARR at $37B growing 123% YoY as the structural engine.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings reported July 13 (confirmed beat); next earnings/Copilot monetization disclosures July 29, 2026 after close; Citi $570 price target outstanding
ideas.edge: 97 analysts avg target ~$590 vs. current ~$400 — ~47% implied upside; forward P/E at multi-year trough (~21–22x NTM); AI ARR $37B growing 123% YoY; post-earnings +3% move on July 15 confirms market receptivity
ideas.bull_case: Valuation re-rating from a 2023-level multiple back toward historical norms would alone close much of the target gap, with Copilot monetization and Azure growth acting as incremental catalysts at each disclosure event.
ideas.bear_case: Hawkish Fed posture pressures growth multiples broadly; if Azure growth decelerates or Copilot monetization disappoints on July 29, the premium multiple becomes a liability despite the cheap-by-history framing.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in all 6 overnight screens, though with a split conviction profile (3 high, 3 medium), reflecting the binary nature of navigating AI valuation fear headlines against a structurally compelling setup. Forward P/E has reset to ~21.7–22x vs. a 5-year average of 72x (Goldman), while 68 analysts carry an average target of ~$303 vs. a current price near ~$207–212, implying ~42–46% upside. TSM's 77% earnings gain confirms AI capex is intact; the sector selloff creates a 40-day swing window into August 26 earnings with guidance of ~$91B already issued.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026 after close; $91B revenue guidance already issued; TSM print confirming AI capex (fresh catalyst as of this week)
ideas.edge: 68-analyst avg target ~$303 vs. ~$207–212 current (~42–46% upside); forward P/E 22x with PEG 0.6 vs. 5-yr avg P/E ~72x; 33 upward EPS revisions vs. 3 downward in last 90 days; 85% revenue growth backdrop
ideas.bull_case: AI capex supercycle confirmed by TSM print; anomalously cheap multiple with 40 days to accumulate into an already-guided $91B quarter — any macro calm could trigger rapid multiple re-expansion toward analyst consensus.
ideas.bear_case: Sector remains vulnerable to AI valuation reset headlines and Gulf/geopolitical noise; elevated absolute price and headline sensitivity could generate sharp drawdowns even without fundamental deterioration, making timing and position sizing critical.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 overnight screens but with the lowest conviction profile (1 high, 5 medium), reflecting genuine upside potential tempered by post-IPO volatility risk. Listed on Nasdaq July 10, 2026 and initiated Overweight by Barclays on July 14 with a target implying ~70–100% upside from current levels (~$186–194), SKHY is the purest liquid US-listed expression of HBM/AI memory scarcity — a structural theme with CEO guidance that memory shortages persist beyond 2030 and HBM supply sold out through 2027. Q2 2026 earnings are expected in late July, adding a near-term binary.
ideas.catalyst: Barclays Overweight initiation July 14, 2026 with ~70–100% upside target (~$255–300 level); SK Hynix Q2 2026 earnings expected late July 2026; South Korea record AI budget announced week of July 10
ideas.edge: IPO priced at $149; current range ~$186–194; Barclays target implies ~$255–300+ (70–100% upside from current); CEO confirmed HBM supply sold out through 2027 and shortage persisting beyond 2030 — structural scarcity with freshly liquid US access
ideas.bull_case: Barclays' 70%+ upside call backed by verifiable supply-demand constraints (HBM sold out through 2027) and a record South Korean AI budget; ADR structure gives US investors clean exposure to a global AI memory duopoly with no listed US-native equivalent.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO volatility is extreme (first-week range $152–$194); bid/ask spreads on options remain wide due to thin liquidity; ADR trades at a premium to Korean shares and could compress; any macro risk-off could disproportionately hit a newly listed, high-beta name.
